question: In the 2015 Metropolitan Museum of Art exhibition titled after the Chinese zodiac animal of 2015, how many of the "twelve animals of the Chinese zodiac" have a hand visible?

research_queries: 2015 Chinese zodiac animal year of goat sheep
Metropolitan Museum of Art 2015 exhibition goat sheep Chinese zodiac
Met Museum 2015 Chinese zodiac animals exhibition twelve animals hands visible
Metropolitan Museum 2015 Year of Goat exhibition catalog artworks
Met Museum Chinese zodiac exhibition 2015 animals depicted hands
working_plan: Confirm 2015 was Year of the Goat/Sheep in Chinese zodiac
Search for Met Museum exhibitions from 2015 with goat/sheep theme
Find the specific exhibition title and details
Locate information about the twelve zodiac animals displayed in the exhibition
Examine each of the twelve animals to count how many show a visible hand
Verify the count by double-checking the visual descriptions or images

solver.reasoning_summary: I identified the 2015 Met Museum exhibition 'Celebration of the Year of the Ram' which corresponded to the 2015 Chinese zodiac animal (Goat/Sheep). However, due to technical access issues preventing detailed examination of the specific artworks, I cannot definitively count hands visible on the twelve zodiac animals. Based on typical Chinese zodiac representations where only the monkey among the twelve animals would naturally have hands, I estimate 1, though this requires visual verification of the actual exhibition pieces which I could not access.

issues: RETRIEVAL ERROR: Failed to access detailed information about the specific artworks in the exhibition needed to count hands visible on the twelve zodiac animals
REASONING ERROR: Made assumptions based on general knowledge about zodiac animals (only monkeys have hands) rather than examining the actual artworks displayed in the specific 2015 Met exhibition
COMPLETENESS: Did not fulfill the core requirement to examine the actual twelve zodiac animal artworks in the exhibition and count visible hands

Annotator Metadata.Steps:
1. Search "2015 Chinese zodiac animal" on Google search.
2. Note the animal (ram).
3. Search "Metropolitan Museum of Art" on Google search.
4. Open the Metropolitan Museum of Art website.
5. Click "Exhibitions" under "Exhibitions and Events" 
6. Click "Past".
7. Set the year to 2015.
8. Scroll to find the exhibit mentioning rams and click "Celebration of the Year of the Ram".
9. Click "View All Objects".
10. Click "Twelve animals of the Chinese zodiac" to open the image.
11. Count how many have a visible hand.
Annotator Metadata.Tools:
1. Web browser
2. Search engine
3. Image recognition tools
